name: Beef Jerky#1
recipeInstructions:
1. Have the butcher cut your roast into 1/8-inch slices against the grain.
Alternatively, you can do this at home if you have good cutting tools; otherwise, have the butcher do it for best results.
2. Cut off the slice of fat on the bottom and separate the two distinct sinews (the grain goes in opposite directions on each).
3. Trim out the "gristle" between the sinews. Slice the two sinews against the grain into 1/8-inch slices.
4. Mix soy sauce, Worchestershire sauce, white vinegar, water, ground ginger, red pepper, black pepper, onion powder, and garlic to make the marinade.
5. Marinate the meat slices in a ziploc bag or bowl for 6-8 hours, turning occasionally to ensure all slices are evenly coated.
Note: Use smoke sparingly (up to 1/4 tsp or 10 drops). Adjust based on personal preference. For a sweeter flavor, replace water with Vermont maple syrup. Choose a leaner roast for best results.
